page.title: Marbella Yacht Charter for Ironman Recovery: Post-Race Relaxation

data.meta_description: Marbella yacht charter for Ironman recovery: post-race relaxation on the Costa del Sol. Charter a boat from Puerto Banús for up to 12 guests. Book now.
data.summary: Discover how a Marbella yacht charter aids Ironman recovery with post-race relaxation on the Costa del Sol. Our fleet departs Puerto Banús, offering licensed skippers, fuel, drinks, and snacks for up to 12 guests.
key_facts: Charter from Puerto Banús, Marbella, with 18 boats accommodating 5–12 guests.
Post-race recovery includes swimming, sunbathing, and gentle cruising along the coast.
All charters include licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and Spanish IVA (21%).
Drinks provided: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, plus light snacks.
Popular recovery spots: Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and the Marbella coastline.
Prices from €749 for 2 hours (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39) to €4,719 for 4 hours (Mangusta 80).
Book via WhatsApp for other boats; no hidden fees.

faq.q: What is the best type of boat for Ironman recovery?
faq.a: A motor yacht with ample deck space and sun loungers, such as the Azimut 58 Flybridge or Mangusta 80, allows you to relax and stretch post-race. Our fleet includes 12.5m to 29m boats, all departing from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: How long should I charter for post-race relaxation?
faq.a: A 4-hour charter is ideal for a gentle cruise, swim stops, and lunch. For deeper relaxation, an 8-hour charter lets you visit multiple coves and enjoy a full day on the water.

faq.q: Are there any specific recovery benefits to a yacht charter?
faq.a: Yes – the gentle motion of the boat, combined with swimming in calm Mediterranean waters and sunbathing, aids muscle recovery. The onboard drinks and snacks keep you hydrated and nourished.

faq.q: Can I bring my own recovery equipment?
faq.a: Absolutely. You're welcome to bring foam rollers, massage balls, or compression gear. Our spacious decks provide room for gentle stretching and self-massage.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden costs.

faq.q: Do you depart from Marbella Marina or only Puerto Banús?
faq.a: All our boats depart from Puerto Banús, the famous marina in Marbella. It's a 10-minute drive from the Ironman finish line, making it convenient for post-race recovery.

faq.q: Can I charter a boat for a group of Ironman finishers?
faq.a: Yes – our larger vessels like the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) or Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) are perfect for groups. Celebrate your achievement together while relaxing on the water.

faq.q: How do I book a yacht for Ironman recovery?
faq.a: Contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing on boats not listed with fixed rates. For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39), book directly at €749/2h or €2,299/8h.

data.body_html:
Crossing the Ironman finish line in Marbella is a monumental achievement. After months of training, your body deserves more than just a cold beer — it needs active recovery. A marbella yacht charter for ironman recovery offers the perfect blend of gentle movement, sea air, and complete relaxation. Our fleet departs from Puerto Banús, just minutes from the race route, and includes everything you need to unwind: licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and insurance — all included in the price.

Why a Yacht Charter Aids Post-Race Recovery

After 226 km of swimming, cycling, and running, your muscles are inflamed and your nervous system is fried. A boat charter provides low-impact recovery: the gentle rocking of the sea reduces cortisol, while swimming in the Mediterranean (average 22°C in autumn) flushes lactic acid. Sunbathing on deck boosts vitamin D, and the onboard drinks — water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava — keep you hydrated. Unlike a cramped hotel room, a yacht gives you space to stretch, lie flat, and breathe.

Our Fleet for Post-Race Relaxation

We operate 18 boats from Puerto Banús, ranging from 6.4m speedboats to 29m superyachts. For Ironman recovery, we recommend vessels with large sun pads and shaded areas. Here are the top picks:

  • Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) — €749/2h or €2,299/8h. Ideal for small groups, with a spacious aft deck for stretching.
  • Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) — pricing on request. Flybridge offers panoramic views and a wind-protected upper deck.
  • Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) — €4,719 minimum 4h (jet ski included). Open-plan layout perfect for group recovery.
  • Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) — pricing on request. The largest in our fleet, with multiple lounging areas.

All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden fees.

Best Coves and Stops for Recovery

Our skippers know the Costa del Sol intimately. For post-race relaxation, they'll recommend calm, sheltered spots where you can swim without currents. Top stops include:

  • Cala del Faro — a quiet cove near Cabopino, with crystal-clear water and minimal boat traffic.
  • Cala Cortés — a sandy beach backed by cliffs, perfect for a gentle swim.
  • Marbella coastline — cruise past the Golden Mile and see La Concha mountain from the sea.
  • Estepona — a longer cruise (about 12 NM from Puerto Banús) for a full-day recovery trip.

All stops are chosen for calm conditions and scenic beauty, not for partying — this is about recovery, not raving.

What to Bring for Optimal Recovery

To make the most of your charter, pack these essentials:

  • Compression gear or loose clothing for changing.
  • Foam roller or massage ball (use on the aft deck).
  • Sunscreen (SPF 50+, the Mediterranean sun is strong even in October).
  • Swimwear for a post-race dip.
  • A towel and a change of clothes.

We provide all drinks and snacks, but you're welcome to bring your own recovery shakes or meals. The skipper can also recommend a nearby restaurant for a post-charter meal.

Pricing and Booking

Our pricing is transparent. For the two most popular recovery boats:

Boat2 Hours8 Hours
Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax)€749€2,299
Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax)€749€2,299

For the Mangusta 80 (any colour), the minimum charter is 4 hours at €4,719, which includes a jet ski. All other boats — including the Azimut 58, Ferretti 94, and Maiora 26m — are priced on request via WhatsApp. Contact us for a custom quote.

Book directly through our Marbella yacht charter hub or send a WhatsApp message for availability.

Practical Information

All charters depart from Puerto Banús, the iconic marina in Marbella. It's a 10-minute drive from the Ironman finish line. Parking is available nearby. The skipper will brief you on safety and itinerary at departure. Boats are equipped with life jackets, first aid kits, and communication devices. Spanish IVA (21%) is included in all prices — no surprises at checkout.

If you're looking for a longer recovery experience, consider a full-day charter to Sotogrande (about 25 NM south) or a sunset cruise along the Golden Mile. Our skippers are flexible and can adjust the route based on your energy levels.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the best type of boat for Ironman recovery?

A motor yacht with ample deck space and sun loungers, such as the Azimut 58 Flybridge or Mangusta 80, allows you to relax and stretch post-race. Our fleet includes 12.5m to 29m boats, all departing from Puerto Banús.

How long should I charter for post-race relaxation?

A 4-hour charter is ideal for a gentle cruise, swim stops, and lunch. For deeper relaxation, an 8-hour charter lets you visit multiple coves and enjoy a full day on the water.

Are there any specific recovery benefits to a yacht charter?

Yes – the gentle motion of the boat, combined with swimming in calm Mediterranean waters and sunbathing, aids muscle recovery. The onboard drinks and snacks keep you hydrated and nourished.

Can I bring my own recovery equipment?

Absolutely. You're welcome to bring foam rollers, massage balls, or compression gear. Our spacious decks provide room for gentle stretching and self-massage.

What is included in the charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden costs.

Do you depart from Marbella Marina or only Puerto Banús?

All our boats depart from Puerto Banús, the famous marina in Marbella. It's a 10-minute drive from the Ironman finish line, making it convenient for post-race recovery.

Can I charter a boat for a group of Ironman finishers?

Yes – our larger vessels like the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) or Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) are perfect for groups. Celebrate your achievement together while relaxing on the water.

How do I book a yacht for Ironman recovery?

Contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing on boats not listed with fixed rates. For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39), book directly at €749/2h or €2,299/8h.
